## 🔐 Security Vulnerability Report

**⚠️ IMPORTANT: For sensitive security issues, please DO NOT post details publicly. Instead, use GitHub's private vulnerability reporting feature or contact the maintainers directly.**

---

## 🔍 Vulnerability Type

**Select the type of security vulnerability:**

- [ ] Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
- [ ] Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
- [ ] SQL Injection
- [ ] Authentication/Authorization Bypass
- [ ] Sensitive Data Exposure
- [ ] Insecure Direct Object References (IDOR)
- [ ] Security Misconfiguration
- [ ] Broken Access Control
- [ ] Insecure Deserialization
- [ ] Using Components with Known Vulnerabilities
- [ ] Inadequate Logging & Monitoring
- [ ] Client-Side Vulnerabilities (HTML/CSS/JavaScript)
- [ ] Other: _______________

---

## 📊 Severity Assessment

**How severe is this vulnerability?**

- [ ] **CRITICAL** - Immediate risk of data breach or complete system compromise
- [ ] **HIGH** - Significant impact on security; could lead to unauthorized access or data loss
- [ ] **MEDIUM** - Notable security risk; could be exploited but with limitations
- [ ] **LOW** - Minor security concern; limited impact if exploited

## ✔️ Checklist

- [ ] I have verified this is a genuine security vulnerability
- [ ] I have not publicly disclosed this vulnerability before reporting
- [ ] I have provided clear reproduction steps
- [ ] I understand the potential impact of this vulnerability
- [ ] I have reviewed existing security issues to avoid duplicates
- [ ] I am willing to work with the maintainers to resolve this issue responsibly

### ⚠️ Important Notice
Please follow responsible disclosure practices. Do not share this vulnerability publicly until the maintainers have had adequate time to address it. Unauthorized disclosure may have legal consequences and puts users at risk.
